title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To define temporal changes in non-immune bone marrow microenvironment populations during myeloid leukaemia progression
context: bcCML LSCs, which can grow in unirradiated mouse recipients (Extended Data Fig. 1a,b)
outcome: Bone marrow stromal populations (leukaemia-CD45-TER119-) were isolated from leukaemic cohorts at distinct stages of disease progression and analysed using scRNA-seq (Fig. 1a).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Gene-expression-based clustering identified 21 lineage clusters with transitional subsets covering endothelial cells, chondrocytes, fibroblasts, pericytes, mesenchymal stromal cells (MSCs) and osteo-associated populations (Fig. 1b and Extended Data Fig. 1c,d) and showed significant remodelling with disease progression (Fig. 1c,d).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our flow-cytometry-based analyses of an independent cohort of leukaemic mice validated these changes, showing increases in MSCs, osteolineage and arteriolar endothelial cells, with a concomitant decline in sinusoidal endothelial populations (Fig. 1e,f and Extended Data Fig. 1e).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Clustering analyses of genes expressed in major lineages identified gene sets with similar changes in expression (Extended Data Fig. 1f)
outcome: suggesting that these may represent altered stromal cell fate.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Pathway analysis showed that osteoblast differentiation and bone mineralization were downregulated in MSCs and osteo-associated cells, and focal adhesion and cell migration were downregulated in endothelial populations
outcome: indicating that MSC and endothelial function may be impaired with disease progression (Fig. 1g and Extended Data Fig. 2a).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Collectively, our temporal analysis of the TME identifies dynamic changes in gene expression of stromal subpopulations during leukaemia progression.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To define the functional relevance of microenvironmental changes on leukaemogenesis
outcome: we focused on proteins expressed on LSC cell surface that act as receptors for niche-driven signals.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To identify LSC receptors associated with disease progression
outcome: we performed RNA-seq analysis of human AML and bcCML CD34+ cells, and healthy donor bone marrow CD34+ haematopoietic stem and progenitor cells (HSPCs; Extended Data Fig. 2b).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: We noted significant (adjusted P (Padj) < 0.05) upregulation of 1,569 genes in bcCML, 2,842 genes in AML and 2,331 genes in both bcCML and AML compared with normal HSPCs.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To identify signals that are functionally relevant for disease progression
context: our genome-wide in vivo leukaemia CRISPR screen (Ref: 6)
outcome: we found cell surface genes (Ref: 19) that drop out by twofold or more

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: This identified 13 cell surface signals common to both bcCML and AML, 18 unique to AML and 7 unique to bcCML. Of these 38 genes, 16 were misannotated as cell surface in the reference dataset (Ref: 19) and were removed from further analysis (Methods and Extended Data Fig. 2c,d).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: identify ligands for these receptors
context: especially those that were significantly upregulated in our TME scRNA-seq data and in the human AML immune microenvironment (Ref: 20)
outcome: We used NicheNet and the published literature

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: This led to the exclusion of receptors with no known ligands (MR1, TMCO3 and TSPAN15) as well as those of which the ligands were not significantly enriched in any TME population (LGALS3BP, CD96, CD274 and CD3D). We therefore generated a unique map of TME ligands for 15 LSC receptors that are essential for disease progression (Fig. 2a).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: whether disease progression could alter the expression of LSC-specific TME ligands

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: identified four distinct patterns of ligand expression. While the expression of some genes remained steady, for example, Jam2 in arteriolar endothelial cells and Ihh in chondrocytes, the expression of Vcam1, Pcdh7 and Il7 was lost in the TME, especially at the end point. Expression of Anxa1 first increased but then declined towards the end. The fourth category of genes was always expressed, but the microenvironmental populations expressing them changed. These included Pvr, Cdo1, Apoe and Agt (Extended Data Fig. 3a–d).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our analysis capturing dynamic changes in the TME identifies signals
outcome: that may be necessary not only at different stages of disease progression, but also those critical during the entire course of disease.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Of the 22 genes upregulated in human LSCs, only low-density lipoprotein receptor (LDLR) and SLC6A6 were significantly associated with poor prognosis in AML (Fig. 2a–c and Extended Data Fig. 4a,b).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: tested the functional requirement of TME-derived ligands of LDLR and SLC6A6 on LSC growth.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: One of the primary LDLR ligands, apolipoprotein E (APOE) (Ref: 21), was highly expressed in MSCs (Extended Data Fig. 3d).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To test the role of APOE from MSCs on LSC function
outcome: we co-cultured bcCML LSCs with MSCs transduced with short hairpin RNAs (shRNAs) targeting Apoe or LacZ (control; Extended Data Fig. 4c).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Co-culture with MSCs lacking Apoe expression led to significant reduction in both the viability and colony-forming ability of LSCs (around 2.1-fold lower than the controls; Extended Data Fig. 4d–f).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our experiments indicate that Slc6a6 expression can be directly induced by oncogenes (Extended Data Fig. 4g).
context: SLC6A6 encodes TAUT, which has high affinity for the non-essential amino acid taurine, and low affinity for β-alanine (taurine Km = 4.5 μM versus β-alanine Km = 56 μM) (Ref: 22).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: While the leukaemia TME did not express enzymes required for β-alanine synthesis (Gadl1 and Cndp1), those needed for taurine biosynthesis were expressed in osteo-associated cells (Cdo1 and Csad; Extended Data Fig. 4h,i).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our analysis of publicly available human bone marrow stromal cell scRNA-seq data using surgical samples from osteoarthritis patients (Ref: 23) showed that CDO1 expression is restricted to MSC and osteolineage populations (Extended Data Fig. 4j).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To test whether CDO1 is expressed in the human leukaemia TME
outcome: we performed an scRNA-seq analysis of CD45- stromal cells from three myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) and AML bone marrow aspirates.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our analysis of the limited numbers of stromal cells in these samples indicate that CDO1 expression is restricted to the MSC/osteolineage cells (Extended Data Fig. 4k,l), as we see in mouse samples.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Notably, we found a marked increase in CDO1 protein expression in nearly all matched biopsies of patients who progressed from MDS to secondary AML, and those who relapsed after AML diagnosis (Fig. 2d–g and Extended Data Fig. 4m–p).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: While CDO1 protein was expressed in undifferentiated human AML MSCs, its expression increased during in vitro mouse and human AML MSC osteogenic differentiation (Extended Data Fig. 5a–c).
outcome: Thus, CDO1 may be broadly expressed in immature MSCs as well as differentiating osteolineage cells in human bone marrow.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: taurine levels in extracellular medium increased during MSC differentiation
outcome: indicating that elevated CDO1 expression is correlated with taurine production (Extended Data Fig. 5d).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To determine a functional role of taurine from osteolineage cells
outcome: we tested the impact of inhibiting taurine biosynthesis in mouse and human leukaemia bone-marrow-derived MSCs on co-cultured leukaemia cells.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our experiments showed that mouse LSCs co-cultured with osteolineage cells lacking Cdo1 expression were significantly less viable (about 2-fold lower than the controls) and formed fewer colonies (3.4-fold less than controls), which could be rescued by supplementing cultures with taurine (Extended Data Fig. 5e–g).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: inhibiting CDO1 in osteolineage cultures from MSCs derived from patients with AML impaired survival and colony-formation of co-cultured AML cells from the same patient by 2- to 3.2-fold (Fig. 2h,i and Extended Data Fig. 5h)
outcome: indicating that taurine produced by osteolineage cells promotes LSC growth and survival.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: taurine levels were 1.7-fold higher in leukaemic bone marrow interstitial fluid as compared to the controls (Fig. 2j).
outcome: Consistent with a functional role of taurine from the TME in leukaemia progression

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To determine whether taurine produced locally in the bone marrow niche is essential for leukaemia growth
context: In mice, the majority of taurine is synthesized in the liver from cysteine (Ref: 24).
outcome: we generated a new Cdo1fl/fl mouse model and crossed it to MSC/osteolineage-specific Prrx1-cre mice (Extended Data Fig. 5i–k). We used these as recipients for LSCs and monitored survival, and the impact on bone marrow microenvironment populations.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our experiments showed that Cdo1fl/flPrrx1-cre+ mice lived around 13.5% longer than the controls
outcome: indicating that taurine produced locally in the leukaemia TME can support disease progression, at least in part (Fig. 2k).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: We did not find any significant changes in the composition of TME in Cdo1fl/flPrrx1-cre+ leukaemic mice as compared to the controls (Extended Data Fig. 5l–n)
outcome: suggesting that leukaemia-driven remodelling of the bone marrow niche does not depend on taurine produced by osteolineage cells.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: whether exogenous taurine supplements can promote leukaemia growth.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our experiments showed that the colony-forming ability of mouse LSCs and patient-derived AML cells increased by 1.2- to 3.3-fold in the presence of taurine (Extended Data Fig. 5o–q).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Taurine supplements could significantly accelerate disease progression in immunocompetent mice (around threefold higher likelihood of death relative to the controls; Extended Data Fig. 5r)
outcome: indicating that taurine can promote leukaemic progression.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: the colony-forming ability of mouse LSCs and patient-derived AML cells increased by 1.2- to 3.3-fold in the presence of taurine... Taurine supplements could significantly accelerate disease progression in immunocompetent mice
outcome: Collectively, these data indicate a key role of taurine and APOE from the bone marrow niche in sustaining LSCs.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: we focused our studies on SLC6A6, as it may be broadly required for growth of aggressive leukaemias.
context: As SLC6A6 expression was significantly enriched in both human bcCML and AML LSCs as compared to the controls

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To determine whether TAUT has a functional role in leukaemia progression

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our experiments showed that TAUT loss in LSCs significantly reduced their ability to grow when co-cultured with osteolineage cells expressing either control shRNA or shRNA against Cdo1 (shCdo1; Extended Data Fig. 6a)
outcome: indicating that they could not respond to taurine being produced by the niche.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: TAUT loss impaired initiation of bcCML in mouse models (3.9-fold higher likelihood of survival; Fig. 3c,d), which could not be rescued with taurine supplements (Extended Data Fig. 6b).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: a 2.3-fold reduction in their colony-forming ability (Fig. 3e), as well as a marked increase in the survival of mice transplanted with Slc6a6-/- LSCs (40%) relative to control LSCs (0%) in secondary transplant assays (21.2-fold higher likelihood of survival; Fig. 3f).
outcome: TAUT loss also led to functional depletion in bcCML LSCs

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: The 8.5-fold reduction in colony-forming ability of bcCML LSCs from secondary transplants
outcome: suggests that these leukaemias remain dependent on TAUT expression and taurine uptake for their continued propagation (Fig. 3g,h).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: We noted no significant differences in bone marrow microenvironmental populations of mice bearing Slc6a6-/- leukaemias as compared to the controls
outcome: indicating that leukaemia-driven niche remodelling possibly reflects the extent of the tumour burden, and may be independent of taurine levels within leukaemia cells (Extended Data Fig. 6c–g).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To determine whether TAUT is broadly required for de novo AML growth
outcome: we tested the impact of its loss on initiation of leukaemia driven by MLL-AF9/NRAS(G12V) and by AML-ETO9a/NRAS(G12V).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our experiments showed that TAUT loss markedly delays the initiation of MLL-driven AML relative to the control (6.1-fold higher likelihood of survival; Fig. 3i–k).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Slc6a6-/- KIT+ AML LSCs from established disease formed 2.9-fold fewer colonies compared with the controls (Fig. 3l)
outcome: indicating that TAUT loss depleted functional LSCs.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: TAUT loss in disease driven by AML-ETO9a resulted in a marked increase in survival (70%) relative to the controls (0%) (36.3-fold higher likelihood of survival; Fig. 3m,n).
outcome: Consistent with a key role of TAUT expression in myeloid leukaemia initiation

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: TAUT loss led to a 2.4- to 3.6-fold reduction in primitive Lin-CD150-FLT3+SCA1+ LSCs (Ref: 6) (Fig. 3o)... increased necrosis and the Lin+ differentiated cells (Fig. 3p and Extended Data Fig. 6h) and promoted cell proliferation (Fig. 3q).
outcome: These data demonstrate a critical requirement for TAUT in the initiation, self-renewal and propagation of myeloid leukaemia.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: the impact of TAUT loss on normal haematopoiesis.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our analysis indicates that TAUT loss does not severely impact bone marrow cellularity, or the total numbers of HSCs, progenitors and differentiated cells at steady-state (Extended Data Fig. 7a–e).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: While TAUT loss did not impair initial HSC engraftment (1 month after transplant), donor chimerism dropped over time (Extended Data Fig. 7f,g).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Although overall bone marrow chimerism 4 months after transplant was 2.4-fold lower in Slc6a6-/- HSC recipients as compared to Slc6a6+/+ HSC recipients, these Slc6a6-/- HSCs were able to contribute to all haematopoietic lineages (Extended Data Fig. 7h–k).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: While TAUT loss did not impair initial HSC engraftment (1 month after transplant), donor chimerism dropped over time... Serial transplantation of Slc6a6+/+ and Slc6a6-/- bone marrow cells showed a similar loss in Slc6a6-/- engraftment over time (Extended Data Fig. 7l–q).
outcome: These results suggest that, while TAUT loss does not impair steady-state haematopoiesis, it can impact long-term HSC self-renewal and maintenance.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: non-genetic approaches using small-molecule inhibitors or gene silencing may identify a therapeutic window for TAUT targeting in human cells.
context: genetic loss of LSC-enriched genes such as CD98 (Ref: 1), STAU2 (Ref: 6), MSI2 (Ref: 28,29), BRD4 (Ref: 30) and BCL2 (Ref: 31) can impair HSC self-renewal. However, therapeutic inhibition of CD98 showed minimal toxicity in AML phase I trials (Ref: 1) and BCL2 inhibitors are approved for AML therapy.
outcome: We therefore tested the impact of TAUT inhibition on growth and proliferation of normal human HSPCs as well as patient-derived AML cells.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: Our analyses of available gene expression datasets indicate that SLC6A6 expression is enriched in leukaemia stem/progenitors as compared to more mature blasts (Extended Data Fig. 8a).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: While SLC6A6 is broadly expressed in AML irrespective of karyotype or FAB subtype (TCGA; Extended Data Fig. 8b), increased expression correlates with venetoclax resistance (BEAT-AML; Fig. 4a).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: SLC6A6 expression is also enriched in reactive oxygen species (ROS)-low LSCs from human monocytic AMLs (subtype clinically associated with venetoclax resistance; CD45brightSSChighCD117-CD11b+CD68+) compared with primitive AML (Fig. 4b; CD45medSSClowCD117+CD11b-CD68-) (Ref: 32).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: We find increased SLC6A6 expression in leukaemia cells carrying RAS mutations as compared to wild-type cells
outcome: consistent with data correlating RAS mutations with venetoclax resistance (Ref: 33) (Fig. 4c).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: SLC6A6 is also highly expressed in relapsed AML originating from stem/progenitor like cells compared with more committed populations (Ref: 34) (Extended Data Fig. 8c).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: increased expression correlates with venetoclax resistance... SLC6A6 expression is also enriched in reactive oxygen species (ROS)-low LSCs from human monocytic AMLs... increased SLC6A6 expression in leukaemia cells carrying RAS mutations... highly expressed in relapsed AML originating from stem/progenitor like cells
outcome: These data collectively indicate that inhibiting SLC6A6 may be of value across AML subtypes, including those commonly associated with venetoclax resistance.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: To determine whether small-molecule inhibitors of TAUT can effectively block leukaemia growth
outcome: we used two well-characterized structural analogues of taurine that inhibit uptake: 6-aminomethyl-3-methyl-4H-1,2,4-benzothiadiazine-1,1-dioxide hydrochloride (Ref: 35) (TAG) and guanidinoethyl sulphonate (Ref: 36) (GES; Extended Data Fig. 8d,e).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: TAG and GES treatment led to a substantial reduction in colonies formed by Slc6a6+/+ mouse LSCs but not Slc6a6-/- cells (Extended Data Fig. 8f–h).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: Importantly, while TAG and GES impaired growth of primary human AML cells in colony assays by 1.8- to 20-fold, they did not impact normal human CD34+ HSPC colony growth (Fig. 4d,e).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: whether venetoclax can exacerbate the impact of TAUT loss and/or inhibition on LSC function.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: Our experiments showed that venetoclax reduced the viability of mouse Slc6a6-/- LSCs by 3.2-fold and lowered their colony formation by 13.2-fold as compared to the controls (Extended Data Fig. 8i,j).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: While TAG, GES or venetoclax alone could impair mouse LSC colony formation by 1.4- to 3.9-fold, their combination was synergistic and led to 2.3- to 7.2-fold fewer colonies as compared to the controls (Extended Data Fig. 8k–m).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: combining the treatments substantially impaired colony formation by 2.4- to 150-fold.
outcome: These data indicate that taurine inhibitors can synergize with venetoclax in blocking the growth of human AML cells (Fig. 4f–h).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: to determine the impact of inhibiting TAUT expression on human AML growth.
context: As TAG and GES did not effectively block taurine uptake in vivo (Extended Data Fig. 9a–j)

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: Knocking down SLC6A6 expression using two independent shRNAs significantly impaired taurine uptake by 2.2 to 3.4-fold, as well as the colony-forming ability of human bcCML, AML and MDS cell lines by 2- to 12-fold (Extended Data Fig. 9k–p).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: Our experiments showed that SLC6A6 knockdown in samples from patients with AML reduced their colony-forming ability by 2.33- to 9.12-fold as compared to the controls (Fig. 4i).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: By contrast, SLC6A6 knockdown did not impact colonies formed by normal human CD34+ HSPCs (Fig. 4j).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
subsection: TAUT is essential for human AML growth
main_content: SLC6A6 knockdown reduced the engraftment of primary AML cells by 1.2- to 40-fold in patient-derived xenograft models (Fig. 4k,l), it did not significantly impair engraftment of normal human CD34+ HSPCs in xenograft models (Fig. 4m).
outcome: Collectively, our data identify a critical requirement for SLC6A6 expression in primary human AML growth and indicate that blocking taurine transport may be of value in aggressive myeloid leukaemias.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To establish the mechanisms by which taurine uptake in leukaemia cells promotes disease progression
outcome: we determined metabolic changes in the absence of taurine.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our untargeted metabolomic analysis of mouse LSCs identified significant downregulation of glycolysis/TCA related pathways and metabolites such as pyruvate, glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate and 3-phosphoglycerate in Slc6a6-/- cells
outcome: suggesting that taurine may regulate energy metabolism (Fig. 5a–c and Extended Data Fig. 10a).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: we noted a 1.3- to 1.8-fold reduction in basal glycolysis, glycolytic capacity, maximal oxygen consumption and spare respiratory capacity in Slc6a6-/- LSCs as compared to the controls (Fig. 5d,e and Extended Data Fig. 10b).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To functionally test the role of glycolysis and TCA associated metabolites
outcome: we determined whether bypassing these could rescue Slc6a6-/- defects.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: the colony-forming ability of Slc6a6-/- LSCs could be significantly rescued by pyruvate, sodium acetate and lactate, but not glucagon (Fig. 5f and Extended Data Fig. 10c–e).
outcome: These data suggest that glycolysis, and not gluconeogenesis, is the primary downstream effector of taurine in leukaemia cells.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: whether taurine contributed to any cellular metabolite
outcome: We next tested... by determining 13C-taurine incorporation in K562 leukaemia cells.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our untargeted metabolomic approach identified 13C label only in known taurine conjugates, N-acetyl taurine and glutaurine (Extended Data Fig. 10f,g).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Membrane-permeable taurine fully rescued colonies formed by Slc6a6-/- cells (Fig. 5g), and N-acetyl-taurine partly rescued this defect (Extended Data Fig. 10h)
outcome: perhaps by breakdown to taurine and acetate

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our untargeted metabolomic approach identified 13C label only in known taurine conjugates, N-acetyl taurine and glutaurine... glutaurine was unable to rescue the Slc6a6-/- colony formation (Extended Data Fig. 10i).
outcome: These experiments indicate that taurine, and not a secondary metabolite, promotes leukaemia growth due to an indirect effect on glycolysis, possibly through downstream signalling.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To identify signals downstream of taurine
outcome: we performed RNA-seq as well as phosphoproteome and total-proteome analyses of primary mouse leukaemia cells (Fig. 5h).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our RNA-seq analysis identified 932 downregulated and 1,158 upregulated genes in the absence of TAUT (Padj < 0.05). The 1,158 upregulated genes included pathways associated with haematopoietic cell lineage and cell cycle regulation (Extended Data Fig. 11a,b). The 932 downregulated genes primarily constituted pathways associated with glycolysis (Fig. 5i and Extended Data Fig. 11b–d)
outcome: consistent with reduced abundance of glycolysis associated metabolites in these cells (Fig. 5c).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: To determine effectors of glycolytic downregulation on TAUT loss
outcome: we performed gene set enrichment analysis.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: This identified profound downregulation of oncogenic MYC and mTOR pathways (Fig. 5j and Extended Data Fig. 11c).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: whether TAUT loss impairs mTOR activation.
context: As mTOR signalling is known to regulate expression of glycolysis related genes (Ref: 38)

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Western blot analysis showed that TAUT loss results in a 2.2- to 3-fold reduction in activated mTOR, p70S6k and pEIF4B as compared to the controls (Fig. 5k–m and Extended Data Fig. 11e).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our global phosphoproteome and total-proteome analyses also showed reduced expression of phosphorylated proteins associated with mTOR signalling, as well as 3.3-fold reduced BCL2 and 1.5-fold increased LC3A expression in the absence of TAUT (Extended Data Fig. 11f,g).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Like TAUT loss, TAUT inhibition with TAG and GES downregulated glycolysis-related gene expression (Extended Data Fig. 11h,i) and impaired phosphorylation of mTOR signalling (Extended Data Fig. 12a,b).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: acute treatment in vitro and long-term taurine dosing in vivo could activate mTOR signalling (Extended Data Fig. 12c–h).
outcome: Consistent with a direct impact of taurine on mTOR signalling

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Inhibiting mTOR with rapamycin impaired the growth of wild-type cells but did not impact Slc6a6-/- cells in vitro or in vivo (Fig. 5n and Extended Data Fig. 12i,k). By contrast, mTOR activation with MHY1485 (Ref: 39) rescued the colony-forming ability of Slc6a6-/- LSCs to 60% of the control, and membrane-permeable taurine rescued mTOR activation (Fig. 5o and Extended Data Fig. 12j–l).
outcome: Collectively, these data indicate that mTOR signalling has a key role downstream of taurine in leukaemia cells.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: We hypothesized that taurine may activate mTOR by promoting its interaction with RAG GTPases.
context: Amino acid availability can be sensed by RAG GTPases to facilitate mTORC1 localization to the outer lysosomal membrane (Ref: 40) and its interaction with RHEB GTPases (Ref: 41), thereby promoting mTORC1 activation.
outcome: To test this, we determined the effect of expressing constitutively active RAGA(Q66L) or RHEB(Q64L) on the colony-forming ability of Slc6a6 wild-type and null cells.

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our experiments showed that, while RHEB(Q64L) partially rescued Slc6a6-/- colony formation, this could be fully rescued by RAGA(Q66L) (Fig. 5p).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Furthermore, mTOR phosphorylation in Slc6a6-/- cells could be rescued by RAGA(Q66L) (Extended Data Fig. 12m).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our immunofluorescence-based analysis showed that, at the baseline, Slc6a6-/- cells have 1.2-fold lower mTOR–LAMP1 interaction, which could be fully rescued by RAGA(Q66L).

title: Taurine from tumour niche drives glycolysis to promote leukaemogenesis
main_content: Our experiments showed that, while RHEB(Q64L) partially rescued Slc6a6-/- colony formation, this could be fully rescued by RAGA(Q66L) (Fig. 5p)... Taurine supplements increased mTOR–LAMP1 co-localization to levels seen in cells expressing RAGA(Q66L).
outcome: Collectively, our studies show that the taurine–TAUT axis regulates glycolysis in myeloid leukaemia cells by promoting RAG-mediated activation of mTOR signalling.
